fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Iowa Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Iowa - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity iowa, how to transfer dietitian license to iowa, dietitian compact iowa, iowa dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Iowa Board of Dietetics
board.parent_dept: Department of Inspections, Appeals, and Licensing (DIAL)

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: The credential required to practice dietetics in Iowa, regulated by the Iowa Board of Dietetics under DIAL.

steps.title: Complete Required Education and Supervised Practice
steps.description: Complete a minimum of a bachelor’s degree from a U.S. regionally accredited college or university or foreign equivalent and complete a supervised practice program.

steps.title: Pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration Examination
steps.description: Successfully complete the Commission on Dietetic Registration examination.

steps.title: Apply for a License
steps.description: Submit a completed application, required documentation, and fees to the Iowa Board of Dietetics.

documents.detail: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
Sent directly to the Board showing your conferred degree.
Documentation satisfying the SAVE program requirements for state licensure.

provisional_text: Iowa does not offer a temporary or provisional license for dietitians. You must complete the CDR examination before a license will be issued.
reciprocity_text: Out-of-state applicants with active CDR registration may apply via endorsement. Verification of licensure from any state where you hold or have held a license is required, along with proof of your CDR credential.

faqs.question: How much does an Iowa dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial cost is a nonrefundable $120 application fee.

faqs.question: When does the Iowa license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: The Iowa dietitian license is renewed biennially (every two years). The renewal fee is $120. A late fee of $60 is applied if renewed after the expiration date.

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements for Iowa?
faqs.answer: Iowa requires 30 hours of dietetics-related continuing education (CE) during each two-year renewal cycle. First-time licensees are exempt from this requirement for their initial renewal.

faqs.question: Does Iowa have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: Iowa has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Iowa?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 3 to 4 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Iowa while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: Out-of-state applicants with active CDR registration may apply via endorsement. Verification of licensure from any state where you hold or have held a license is required, along with proof of your CDR credential.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Iowa
hero.dek: Iowa Dietitian License requires a completed application, passing the Commission on Dietetic Registration examination, and payment of fees.
